Scenario.title: Transportation System Phishing and Denial of Service Attack
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple A basic-skilled threat group leverages phishing and unpatched software vulnerabilities to disrupt transportation traffic control systems through denial-of-service attacks.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Target System Artifact, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Exploit TEE Vulnerability, Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends fraudulent emails impersonating official maintenance alerts or ticketing systems, tricking employees into clicking malicious links hosted on internet-facing assets.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-67: String Format Overflow in syslog()
stage.description: The attacker exploits known vulnerabilities in outdated ticketing systems (target system artifacts) to execute arbitrary code and gain initial foothold.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker floods traffic control systems with malformed network packets, overwhelming infrastructure and causing operational disruptions.

Scenario.summary: A basic-skilled adversary uses phishing to compromise transportation systems, exploits unpatched software for execution, and deploys denial-of-service attacks to disrupt critical operations.
